methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $5.3B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $6.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Abridge, a Series E biotech and health-tech company, is valued at $5.3B post-money after raising $1.2B+ to date. The valuation analysis relies on a peer universe of 11 comparable companies across the sector: 3 public equities (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private firms including Tempus AI ($6.0B), Ultima Genomics ($4.0B), and Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to the $5.3B last post-money valuation, applies a typical 1.4x Series E step-up multiplier, implying $7.4B. Peer-median analysis across 10 same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $2.3B, with a range spanning $1.5B to $6.0B; this cohort includes both earlier-stage privates and more mature public companies. Public-comp anchoring using a 7.8x P/S median observed across the three public tickers cannot be applied due to lack of disclosed revenue for the target.

The triangulated valuation range is $2.0B to $8.5B, with a median of $4.9B. The current $5.3B valuation sits near the midpoint of this range but above the peer median, reflecting Abridge's advanced stage and capital intensity. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory (unavailable for comparison) and the validity of the 1.4x stage multiplier given recent venture market compression. The wide gap between peer-median ($2.3B) and stage-progression ($7.4B) suggests material sensitivity to methodology selection.
